name: base32g-envelope-20260722
question: Resolve the base32g-on-stock contradiction (2026-07-19 FAIL vs 2026-07-21 OK), and map the real font x size x width envelope.

contradiction_resolved.finding: All four disputed cells (Courier 8pt, widths 60 and max, clean and blur 0.6) now RESTORE on stock, where 2026-07-19 recorded FAIL for every one.
contradiction_resolved.cause: The format work landed between the two dates - decode hardening, machine-frame per-line RS, and the runt-final-line padding fix - moved base32g from model-required to stock-viable. The 2026-07-19 record is superseded, not contradicted: it was measured on an older format.
contradiction_resolved.significance: base32g needs no trained model. Combined with base16g (never needed one) this means NO shipped codec requires an OCR model.

envelope.headline: The limit is the FONT, not the size. Courier restores at every size tested (4-10pt); OCR-B and DejaVu Sans Mono fail at EVERY size.
envelope.courier: OK at 4/5/6/8/10pt width 60, and 5/6/8/10pt width 90. Only failure: 4pt width 90 (smallest glyphs at the widest row). 10pt width 90 is not-built - geometry refuses it (width 90 exceeds the 72-char fit).
envelope.ocr_b: FAIL at every tested size (4,5,6,8pt); 6/8/10pt width 90 and 10pt width 60 not-built on geometry.
envelope.dejavu_sans_mono: FAIL at every tested size and width.
envelope.interpretation: This matches the 2026-07-21 per-glyph drop-rate measurement, which found OCR-B reads a punctuation-heavy alphabet markedly worse than Courier at 8pt. base32g's alphabet adds ?@!&+= to the base16g set, and those are exactly the glyphs that suffer. Failures are dominated by 'cannot recover RS parameters: data/parity line counts are inconsistent' - i.e. LINE-COUNT errors, the same lethal mechanism seen across every failure investigated on 2026-07-21.

correction: An earlier statement in this investigation that base32g's envelope was merely 'thin' (only Courier 8pt measured) understated the issue. It is not thin, it is font-bound: base32g is a COURIER-ONLY codec on stock OCR. Recommending base32g without naming the font would be wrong.
recommendation: base32g is safe to recommend on stock OCR for Courier at 4-10pt (width 60; width 90 works from 5pt up). Do NOT recommend it for OCR-B or DejaVu Sans Mono at any size - use base16g there. Any future denser-codec work must gate per-font, not per-size.
